description: The strength to act with integrity despite fear. This is not the courage of aggression, but the moral bravery to be vulnerable, to speak compassionate truth, and to act for the collective good even when it is difficult.
rationale: A composition of the roots 'ko' (core, center) and 'a' (openness). The word suggests an 'open core' or 'open heart.' The firm stop consonant 'k' provides a sense of strength and resolve, while the vowel hiatus 'o.a' opens into vulnerability and presence. It sonically represents acting from a place of centered, open strength.
